text: Check the logrotate config for this app: `cat /etc/logrotate.d/billing`. Compare the path it names against what's actually there: `ls /var/log/billing/`.

The config points at a filename that doesn't exist anymore -- the app's log file was renamed at some point, and the logrotate config was never updated. `missingok` in the config is exactly why this fails completely silently (no error, no warning, just nothing happening).
Fix the path in `/etc/logrotate.d/billing` to match the real, current log file.

Verify by forcing a run rather than waiting for the schedule: `sudo logrotate --force /etc/logrotate.d/billing`, then check that a rotated file (`billing.log.1`) actually appeared and the live log was truncated.
